toolUseResult.oldString:
# Submit a Cloud Build probe to a freshly-levied worker pool.
#
# Three concurrent effects per pool:
#   1. Submission materializes the pool's per-resource quota row. On fresh
#      levy the quota row is absent and the submission is refused with
#      HTTP 400 — that refusal IS the materialization side-effect, not a
#      failure to surface.
#   2. In-build curl steps assert the pool's intended egress posture
#      (tether: public + Google reachable; airgap: public blocked + Google
#      reachable). Assertion failure surfaces in the build's terminal
#      status as a hard infra-drift signal.
#   3. The docker step exercises GAR write authority by pushing a tiny
#      FROM-scratch marker image to rbi_df under a filename the hallmark /
#      reliquary / enshrinement enumerators ignore.
#
# Builder images: Google-hosted only (gcr.io/...). The airgap pool cannot
# reach docker.io, so anything pulled from there would hang the build.
#
# Submission is fire-and-forget — the build runs async; operators read
# pass/fail from the Cloud Build console link emitted in the probe block.
zrbgp_pool_probe_submit() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1}"
  local -r z_pool_variant="${2}"
  local -r z_pool_id="${3}"
  local -r z_mason_email="${4}"
  local -r z_egress_script="${5}"

  local -r z_region="${RBRR_GCP_REGION}"
  local -r z_pool_resource="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${z_region}/workerPools/${z_pool_id}"
  local -r z_mason_sa="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/${z_mason_email}"
  local -r z_probe_ref="${z_region}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS}/probe-${z_pool_variant}:probe"

  local z_push_script
  z_push_script=$(cat <<PUSH
set -euo pipefail
mkdir -p /workspace/probe
cat > /workspace/probe/probe-marker.txt <<MARKER
RecipeBottle depot levy probe
pool:    ${z_pool_id}
variant: ${z_pool_variant}
depot:   ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}
MARKER
cat > /workspace/probe/Dockerfile <<DF
FROM scratch
COPY probe-marker.txt /
DF
docker build -t "${z_probe_ref}" /workspace/probe
docker push "${z_probe_ref}"
echo "GAR push OK: ${z_probe_ref}"
PUSH
) || buc_die "Failed to compose probe push script for ${z_pool_variant}"

  local -r z_build_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_probe_${z_pool_variant}.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg posture_image   "gcr.io/google.com/cloudsdktool/cloud-sdk:alpine" \
    --arg docker_image    "gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker" \
    --arg egress_script   "${z_egress_script}" \
    --arg push_script     "${z_push_script}" \
    --arg pool_resource   "${z_pool_resource}" \
    --arg service_account "${z_mason_sa}" \
    '{
      steps: [
        {
          name: $posture_image,
          id: "assert-egress-posture",
          entrypoint: "bash",
          args: ["-c", $egress_script]
        },
        {
          name: $docker_image,
          id: "probe-gar-push",
          entrypoint: "bash",
          args: ["-c", $push_script]
        }
      ],
      serviceAccount: $service_account,
      options: {
        logging: "CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY",
        pool: { name: $pool_resource }
      }
    }' > "${z_build_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to compose probe build JSON for ${z_pool_variant}"

  local -r z_build_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}${RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${z_region}/builds"
  local -r z_infix="depot_probe_${z_pool_variant}_submit"

  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_build_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}" "${z_build_file}"
  local z_submit_code
  z_submit_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_infix}") \
    || buc_die "Bad probe submission HTTP code for ${z_pool_variant}"

  buc_info "Per-pool probe: ${z_pool_variant}"
  buc_info "  Pool:     ${z_pool_id}"
  buc_info "  Artifact: ${z_probe_ref}"

  case "${z_submit_code}" in
    200|201)
      local z_build_id=""
      z_build_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.metadata.build.id') || z_build_id=""
      if test -n "${z_build_id}"; then
        buc_info "  Build:    ${z_build_id} (submitted — runs async)"
        buc_link "  " "Open probe build in Cloud Console" \
          "${RBGC_CONSOLE_URL}cloud-build/builds;region=${z_region}/${z_build_id}?project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
      else
        buc_info "  Build:    submitted (HTTP ${z_submit_code} — build ID not in response)"
      fi
      ;;
    400)
      buc_info "  Build:    HTTP 400 — quota row materialized (expected on fresh levy)"
      buc_info "  Hint:     Request a quota increase via the depot Cloud Build console, then re-run levy to exercise the probe end-to-end"
      ;;
    *)
      local z_err
      z_err=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.error.message') || z_err="HTTP ${z_submit_code}"
      buc_die "Probe submission failed for ${z_pool_variant}: ${z_err}"
      ;;
  esac
}
toolUseResult.newString:
# Submit a Cloud Build probe to a freshly-levied worker pool. Three effects:
# submission materializes the pool's per-resource quota row (HTTP 400 on
# fresh levy is the expected side-effect, not a failure to surface);
# in-build curl asserts egress posture; docker step pushes a tiny
# FROM-scratch marker to rbi_df under a filename the enumerators ignore.
# Builder images are Google-hosted only — airgap cannot reach docker.io.
# Fire-and-forget — operators read pass/fail from the console link.
# Args: token pool_variant pool_id mason_email egress_script_file
zrbgp_pool_probe_submit() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1}"
  local -r z_pool_variant="${2}"
  local -r z_pool_id="${3}"
  local -r z_mason_email="${4}"
  local -r z_egress_script_file="${5}"

  local -r z_region="${RBRR_GCP_REGION}"
  local -r z_pool_resource="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${z_region}/workerPools/${z_pool_id}"
  local -r z_mason_sa="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/${z_mason_email}"
  local -r z_probe_ref="${z_region}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS}/probe-${z_pool_variant}:probe"

  # Push script — pool-specific values baked in at host-side, so Cloud Build
  # sees a fully-resolved script with no substitutions. Heredocs inside this
  # file are cloud-side bash (executed by the build worker); BCG host-side
  # heredoc prohibition does not apply to content destined for cloud execution.
  local -r z_push_script_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_probe_${z_pool_variant}_push.sh"
  printf '%s\n' \
    '#!/bin/bash' \
    'set -euo pipefail' \
    'mkdir -p /workspace/probe' \
    'cat > /workspace/probe/probe-marker.txt <<MARKER' \
    'RecipeBottle depot levy probe' \
    "pool:    ${z_pool_id}" \
    "variant: ${z_pool_variant}" \
    "depot:   ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" \
    'MARKER' \
    'cat > /workspace/probe/Dockerfile <<DF' \
    'FROM scratch' \
    'COPY probe-marker.txt /' \
    'DF' \
    "docker build -t \"${z_probe_ref}\" /workspace/probe" \
    "docker push \"${z_probe_ref}\"" \
    "echo \"GAR push OK: ${z_probe_ref}\"" \
    > "${z_push_script_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write probe push script for ${z_pool_variant}"

  local -r z_build_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_probe_${z_pool_variant}_build.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg     posture_image   "gcr.io/google.com/cloudsdktool/cloud-sdk:alpine" \
    --arg     docker_image    "gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker" \
    --rawfile egress_script   "${z_egress_script_file}" \
    --rawfile push_script     "${z_push_script_file}" \
    --arg     pool_resource   "${z_pool_resource}" \
    --arg     service_account "${z_mason_sa}" \
    '{
      steps: [
        {
          name:   $posture_image,
          id:     "assert-egress-posture",
          script: $egress_script
        },
        {
          name:   $docker_image,
          id:     "probe-gar-push",
          script: $push_script
        }
      ],
      serviceAccount: $service_account,
      options: {
        logging: "CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY",
        pool: { name: $pool_resource }
      }
    }' > "${z_build_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to compose probe build JSON for ${z_pool_variant}"

  local -r z_build_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}${RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${z_region}/builds"
  local -r z_infix="depot_probe_${z_pool_variant}_submit"

  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_build_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}" "${z_build_file}"
  local z_submit_code
  z_submit_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_infix}") \
    || buc_die "Bad probe submission HTTP code for ${z_pool_variant}"

  buc_info "Per-pool probe: ${z_pool_variant}"
  buc_info "  Pool:     ${z_pool_id}"
  buc_info "  Artifact: ${z_probe_ref}"

  case "${z_submit_code}" in
    200|201)
      local z_build_id
      z_build_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.metadata.build.id') || z_build_id=""
      if test -n "${z_build_id}"; then
        buc_info "  Build:    ${z_build_id} (submitted — runs async)"
        buc_link "  " "Open probe build in Cloud Console" \
          "${RBGC_CONSOLE_URL}cloud-build/builds;region=${z_region}/${z_build_id}?project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
      else
        buc_info "  Build:    submitted (HTTP ${z_submit_code} — build ID not in response)"
      fi
      ;;
    400)
      buc_info "  Build:    HTTP 400 — quota row materialized (expected on fresh levy)"
      buc_info "  Hint:     Request a quota increase via the depot Cloud Build console, then re-run levy to exercise the probe end-to-end"
      ;;
    *)
      local z_err
      z_err=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.error.message') || z_err="HTTP ${z_submit_code}"
      buc_die "Probe submission failed for ${z_pool_variant}: ${z_err}"
      ;;
  esac
}

input.old_string:
buc_step 'Submit per-pool probe builds (materialize quota rows + assert egress posture + GAR write)'

  # Tether posture: public reachable AND Google reachable.
  local -r z_tether_egress_script=$(cat <<'TETHER'
set -euo pipefail
curl -fsS --max-time 10 https://example.com > /dev/null
echo "tether: public reachable OK"
curl -fsS --max-time 10 https://storage.googleapis.com > /dev/null
echo "tether: google reachable OK"
TETHER
)

  # Airgap posture: public BLOCKED AND Google reachable. Public-reachable is
  # a hard infra-drift signal — surfaces as build failure on the assertion.
  local -r z_airgap_egress_script=$(cat <<'AIRGAP'
set -euo pipefail
if curl -fsS --max-time 10 https://example.com > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "airgap: ERROR public is reachable (expected blocked)" >&2
  exit 1
fi
echo "airgap: public blocked OK"
curl -fsS --max-time 10 https://storage.googleapis.com > /dev/null
echo "airgap: google reachable OK"
AIRGAP
)

  zrbgp_pool_probe_submit "${z_token}" "tether" "${z_tether_id}" "${z_mason_sa_email}" "${z_tether_egress_script}"
  zrbgp_pool_probe_submit "${z_token}" "airgap" "${z_airgap_id}" "${z_mason_sa_email}" "${z_airgap_egress_script}"

input.new_string:
//axhos_step
. *Enable Cloud Build service agent to impersonate
{rbtr_mason}*:
.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa} on `«MASON_EMAIL»`
.. Add member: `serviceAccount:service-«PROJECT_NUMBER»@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.iam.gserviceaccount.com`
.. Grant role: `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator`
.. NOTE: Cloud Build service agent is Google-provisioned (auto-created when Cloud Build API is enabled), not a user-created service account. The `tokenCreator` role enables build steps running as {rbtr_mason} (via the build JSON's `serviceAccount` field) to generate fresh access tokens for GCP API calls — required for GAR authentication (docker login, crane push). This is distinct from `serviceAccountUser` (actAs), which the {rbtr_director} holds on {rbtr_mason} for submitting builds.
.. {rbbc_require}
IAM binding successful

//axhos_step
. *Submit Per-Pool Probe Builds*:
.. For each worker pool variant in [`tether`, `airgap`]:
... {rbbc_submit}
link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/create[builds.create] with:
.... `serviceAccount`: `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/serviceAccounts/«MASON_EMAIL»`
.... `options.pool.name`: full pool resource path for the variant
.... `options.logging`: `CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY`
.... `steps[0]`: egress-posture assertion (builder: `gcr.io/google.com/cloudsdktool/cloud-sdk:alpine`)
..... tether asserts `https://example.com` reachable AND `https://storage.googleapis.com` reachable
..... airgap asserts `https://example.com` blocked AND `https://storage.googleapis.com` reachable
.... `steps[1]`: GAR write probe (builder: `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker`) — `FROM scratch` image with a single marker file, pushed to `«REGION»-docker.pkg.dev/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/«GAR_REPOSITORY»/rbi_df/probe-«VARIANT»:probe`
... {rbbc_require}
submission returns HTTP 200/201 (build accepted) OR HTTP 400 (per-pool quota row absent — see below). All other codes are fatal.
... {rbbc_show}
per-pool probe block carrying the pool ID, GAR artifact reference, and either the build ID + Cloud Build console link (HTTP 200/201) or a quota-row-materialized notice (HTTP 400).
.. NOTE: Builder images are Google-hosted only (`gcr.io/...`). The airgap pool cannot reach `docker.io`; any non-Google builder would hang the build.
.. NOTE: On fresh levy the pool's per-resource concurrent-build quota row does not yet exist. Cloud Build's quota gate refuses the submission with HTTP 400, and that refusal IS the materialization side-effect that creates the quota row — not a failure to surface. The operator can subsequently request a quota increase against the now-existing row, then re-run levy (or any subsequent build submission) to exercise the probe end-to-end.
.. NOTE: Submission is non-blocking; the build runs asynchronously. In-build assertion failure (e.g., airgap pool reaching the public internet) surfaces in the build's terminal status as a hard infra-drift signal — operators inspect via the console link in the probe block.
.. NOTE: The `rbi_df` namespace is a flat sibling of `rbi_hm` / `rbi_rq` / `rbi_es` under the GAR repository. {rbtgo_image_audit} / {rbtgo_image_rekon} enumerators ignore it by design — probe artifacts are operational, not part of the hallmark / reliquary / enshrine image catalogue. Filenames under `rbi_df` form a flat sea (no subdirs); each filename names one depot-scoped OCI artifact.

//axhoot_typed_output

content.description: Add rbi_df constant to rbgc_Constants.sh alongside existing hm/rq/es siblings, with inhabitant-comment block (probe today; future filenames = RBRD variable names; flat layout, no subdirs)

content.subject: Add per-pool probe submission to rbgp_depot_levy
content.description: Insert a new step in rbgp_depot_levy that submits a builds.create probe per pool (tether + airgap). Probe: egress-posture assertion + GAR push to rbi_df. content.text:
## Pace ₢BOAAD work complete — ready for review

**Files modified:**
- `Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh` — added `RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS="rbi_df"` with inhabitant documentation block
- `Tools/rbk/rbgl_GarLayout.sh` — added `RBGL_DEPOT_FACTS_ROOT`
- `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh` — added `zrbgp_pool_probe_submit` private helper, wired two call sites (tether + airgap) into `rbgp_depot_levy` between the Mason-impersonation grant and the depot-tracking update
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc` — added "Submit Per-Pool Probe Builds" axhos_step

**Probe contract per pool:**
- Builder images Google-hosted only (`gcr.io/google.com/cloudsdktool/cloud-sdk:alpine` for egress, `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker` for push) — airgap-compatible
- Step 1 asserts pool's intended egress posture (tether: public + Google reachable; airgap: public blocked + Google reachable)
- Step 2 pushes a `FROM scratch` marker image to `{region}-docker.pkg.dev/{depot}/{repo}/rbi_df/probe-{variant}:probe`
- HTTP 400 on fresh-levy is tolerated and reported as expected quota-row materialization; the per-pool probe block emits pool ID, artifact ref, build ID + Cloud Console link (200/201) OR quota-row-materialized notice (400)

**BCG compliance:** `printf '%s\n'` to temp file + `jq --rawfile` (matches the existing rbfc/rbfd/rbfl pattern); host-side bash has no heredocs; two-line capture pattern with `|| buc_die`; `local -r` defaults; `test` over `[[ ]]`; no stderr suppression on host side.

**Validation:**
- `bash -n` on all 3 modified shell files — pass
- `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` — pass
- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` — 72/75 pass; 1 **pre-existing** failure unrelated to changes: `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` complains "RBRV_RELIQUARY must not be empty" — that's a post-Marshal-Zero state in the vessel `rbrv.env` files (e.g., `rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/rbrv.env` has `RBRV_RELIQUARY=`). My diff doesn't touch RBRV or vessel envs.

**Not exercised:** End-to-end probe submission requires an actual `rbw-dL` run against a fresh depot (billable GCP infra + credentials). The gauntlet shakedown moniker `canest2bhl100011` is set up for this when you choose to drive it.

**Next steps for you:**
1. Review the diff
2. Decide whether to drive a depot levy to exercise the probes end-to-end (separately from this pace's wrap)
3. When ready, wrap ₢BOAAD

content.text:
No reliquaries inscribed yet. To conjure busybox, I need to drive the full prerequisite chain: **inscribe** (mirrors 6 tool images to GAR via Cloud Build — uses tether pool) → **yoke** (writes the stamp into every vessel's `rbrv.env`) → **conjure busybox**. Each Cloud Build hits the now-approved 96-core quota.

Starting with the inscribe. This spawns multiple Cloud Builds and takes ~10–15 min total.

content.text:
**Busybox conjure complete:**
- Conjure: SUCCESS in 1m 33s (build `a8ca6024`)
- Vouch: SUCCESS in 0m 59s (build `1a092526`)
- Hallmark minted: `c260515152058-r260515152101`
- 96-core quota is live and the full ordain pipeline (multi-platform conjure → SLSA vouch) works end-to-end against `cancbhl-d-canest2bhl100011`.

---

## Musing: RBRD facts in rbi_df instead of GCS

**One concrete problem with uBuN's current plan:** the depot build bucket is created with a 1-day lifecycle delete (`rbgp_Payor.sh:847` — `condition: { age: 1 }`). A `gs://<depot-bucket>/depot-manifest.json` would be **reaped after 24 hours**. uBuN's locked decision "Manifest lives in GCS" didn't surface this — it would need either a separate manifest-bucket (more infra) or a lifecycle exclusion (fragile, prefix-based).

**Substrate alignment with this pace:** ₢BOAAD already minted `rbi_df` with the explicit intent that future RBRD variables would inhabit it as flat-sea files, each filename = an RBRD variable, content = its assignment. uBuN's facts ARE the RBRD variables it's about to mint. So uBuN doesn't need to introduce a new substrate — it inherits `rbi_df`.

**Substrate-shift implications:**

| Dimension | uBuN as written (GCS) | RBRD-in-rbi_df |
|-----------|----------------------|----------------|
| Write granularity | 1 JSON file | N OCI artifacts (one per RBRD var) |
| Persistence | depot bucket 1-day reap (problem!) | GAR cleanup policy ignores tagged manifests — durable |
| Access path | GCS read at kindle | GAR read at kindle (already in director-scope auth) |
| Drift comparison | hash-gated fast-path | per-fact comparison (more granular drift report; slower) |
| Atomicity at write | one PUT | N pushes (partial-write failure mode) |
| Conflicts with other rbi_df uses | n/a | shares namespace with probes (no path collision — probe filenames are `probe-tether`/`probe-airgap`, fact filenames are `RBRD_*`) |

**Shape sketch (not commitment):**
- At `depot_levy`: emit a single Cloud Build with N steps, each pushing one fact artifact to `rbi_df/RBRD_<VAR>:current`. Content = the variable's assigned value (a literal string, wrapped as a `FROM scratch` + `COPY value /` image — the same shape my probe uses). One build, atomic-on-success.
- At kindle (commands sourcing RBRD): enumerate `rbi_df/RBRD_*` via GAR REST API, fetch each blob, compare to local RBRD env. Mismatch → diff + die. ~4 fetches per session start ≈ 400ms — acceptable.

**Trade-off that needs an explicit decision in uBuN:** the hash-gated fast-path is the elegance of the GCS-manifest plan (one fetch, one hash compare). With per-fact storage, you either (a) drop the fast-path (slower kindle, accept it for one-shot session check), or (b) keep an additional `rbi_df/RBRD_MANIFEST_HASH:current` fact alongside individual fact artifacts — fast-path checks just that one, slow-path enumerates on mismatch.

**Recommended:** when ₣BO retires, slate a reslate of uBuN's Axis B paddock to:
1. Acknowledge the bucket-lifecycle reap problem
2. Switch substrate to `rbi_df` (no separate substrate to mint — already exists)
3. Decide hash-fast-path vs per-fact-on-kindle

Want me to leave this as a `jji_itch` for now, or draft a paddock reslate when this heat wraps?

message.content: Whoa... back up.  You were trying to think 10 steps ahead and I think your figurative step 2 wasn't right.  Do we need to change a retention policy to keep the depot fact files resident?  Those aren't even manifests...  Ah maybe that is the issue, this is GAR not bucket.  Hmm.  State clearly a repair here.  No more talk of slating paces to update dockets, that is crazy.

content.text:
You're right — I conflated the GCS depot bucket with the GAR `rbi_df` namespace. Retracting that framing.

**Repair:**

- Facts in `rbi_df` are tagged OCI artifacts in **GAR**, not objects in the depot **GCS bucket**. The 1-day lifecycle on the bucket has nothing to do with this.
- GAR's only reaping mechanism is the cleanup policy on the depot's repository: `rb-delete-untagged` deletes **UNTAGGED** manifests older than 24h (`rbgc_Constants.sh:214` / applied at `rbgp_Payor.sh:867`). Tagged manifests are kept indefinitely.
- A fact artifact at `rbi_df/RBRD_<VAR>:current` (or any stable tag) **persists indefinitely** with no retention-policy change. The existing cleanup policy is correct as-is — it just reaps orphaned untagged stragglers, which is what we want.

So the "facts get reaped" concern was bogus. No repair needed to retention; the substrate just works.

You're correct that depot facts aren't OCI manifests in the conceptual sense — they're tiny scalar values. Putting them in GAR means each one is wrapped as a single-layer `FROM scratch` image whose layer content carries the value. That wrapping is overhead but cheap, and tagged artifacts persist regardless.

message.content: I need to wind down this chat soon.  Should we update the RBRD pace given our work here?  What is simplest?

content.text:
**Yes, one small edit.**

The `Manifest lives in GCS` locked decision in uBuN is stale-by-mount — the rationale (vs project labels / repo description / Secret Manager) didn't include `rbi_df`, which now exists. At mount-time an agent reading the paddock would see "Manifest lives in GCS" as locked and proceed accordingly, missing the rbi_df option.

**Simplest repair:** replace that one bullet under "Locked decisions" in `jjp_uBuN.md` with text that's still true:

> **Manifest substrate undecided.** At mount-time, choose between a GCS object in a non-build bucket and per-variable artifacts under `rbi_df` (the GAR depot-facts namespace, added in ₣BO; tagged artifacts persist indefinitely). The original GCS rationale held against project labels (charset/length), GAR repo description (clobber risk), and Secret Manager (IAM overhead) — but predated the `rbi_df` namespace existing as a depot-scoped OCI-artifact home.

That single bullet replacement keeps the rest of uBuN intact, surfaces the option at mount-time, and doesn't touch paces. Want me to apply it?

## Locked decisions

- **Burn the bridges — no migration.** RBRD applies to new depots only. Existing depots remain on the pre-RBRD code path until decommissioned. No dual-read, no compatibility shim, no `RBRR_` fallback for the four relocated names.
- **Conversion deferred.** Migrating an existing depot to RBRD is its own future heat, only built on demonstrated need. The plausible mechanism (unmake + relevy preserving payload) is mentioned here for closure, not scoped here.
- **Manifest lives in GCS.** Not project labels (charset-restricted, 64-char value cap), not GAR repo description (single-string clobber risk), not Secret Manager (IAM overhead for non-sensitive data). Plain JSON in the depot's own bucket.
- **Hash over canonical form.** SHA256 over sorted-key, whitespace-deterministic serialization. Hash gates the fast-path check; the stored manifest body provides the diff message on mismatch.
- **Kindle-time enforcement, not per-command.** One fetch per workbench session, regardless of how many commands run.
- **Both axes ship together.** The tripwire could in principle ship alone (snapshotting current RBRR names), but the rename is the user-clarity half — shipping only the tripwire keeps "RBRR means live" still false. Ship as one heat.

## What done looks like

The original GCS rationale held against project labels (charset/length), GAR repo description (clobber risk), and Secret Manager (IAM overhead) — but predated the `rbi_df` namespace existing as a depot-scoped OCI-artifact home.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
## Goal

Make depot-time-immutable settings legible and enforceable. Today, four RBRR settings are read at depot levy and baked into Google Cloud resources; altering them post-levy is a silent no-op that confuses users. Close this gap with two coordinated changes: a renaming that makes the lifecycle visible in the name, and a tripwire that fails loudly on drift.

## The four depot-time settings

Read at depot levy, baked into persistent GCP resources, immutable thereafter without relevying:

- `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` — composed into project ID, GAR repo name, pool stem, GCS bucket name (`rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh`)
- `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` — same composition as above (`rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh`)
- `RBRR_GCP_REGION` — embedded in worker pool path, GCS bucket location, GAR location (`rbgp_Payor.sh` depot-levy path)
- `RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE` — baked into tether and airgap worker pool `workerConfig.machineType` (`rbgp_Payor.sh` depot-levy path)

The remaining eight RBRR settings are live (re-read per operation) and stay where they are: `RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT`, `RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS`, `RBRR_VESSEL_DIR`, `RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE`, `RBRR_DNS_SERVER`, `RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX`, `RBRR_SECRETS_DIR`, `RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL`.

## Axis A — Mint RBRD regime

Relocate the four depot-time settings out of RBRR (which today implies "live") into a new RBRD ("depot regime") whose name communicates lifecycle. Parallel structure to existing regimes:

- `Tools/rbk/rbrd_regime.sh` — enrollments
- `Tools/rbk/rbrd_cli.sh` — render/validate CLI partner
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRD-RegimeDepot.adoc` — spec doc parallel to RBSRP / RBSRR
- Tabtargets `rbw-rdr` / `rbw-rdv` for render/validate
- Variable rename: `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` → `RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX`, etc.
- Lifecycle marshal (`rblm_cli.sh`) updated to emit RBRD blanks alongside RBRR

Why a new regime rather than folding into RBRP: payor identity (who pays, who authenticates) and depot shape (what gets levied) are different lifecycle categories. Conflating them muddles the operator's mental model. A regime named "depot" carries the immutability semantics in the name itself.

## Axis B — GCS manifest tripwire

At depot levy, write `gs://<depot-bucket>/depot-manifest.json` containing the four RBRD values in canonical order, a SHA256 hash over that canonical form, and the levy timestamp.

At kindle time on any command that sources RBRD, fetch the manifest, recompute the hash from the operator's loaded RBRD env, compare. On mismatch:

- Dump side-by-side diff of stored-vs-local values
- Fail with explanatory message naming which fields drifted and stating that the depot must be relevied (or the local change reverted) before any operation can proceed

Enforcement choke point: the kindle path that already sources the regime files. One fetch, one comparison, gates every downstream command in the session.

## Locked decisions

- **Burn the bridges — no migration.** RBRD applies to new depots only. Existing depots remain on the pre-RBRD code path until decommissioned. No dual-read, no compatibility shim, no `RBRR_` fallback for the four relocated names.
- **Conversion deferred.** Migrating an existing depot to RBRD is its own future heat, only built on demonstrated need. The plausible mechanism (unmake + relevy preserving payload) is mentioned here for closure, not scoped here.
- **Manifest lives in GCS.** Not project labels (charset-restricted, 64-char value cap), not GAR repo description (single-string clobber risk), not Secret Manager (IAM overhead for non-sensitive data). Plain JSON in the depot's own bucket.
- **Hash over canonical form.** SHA256 over sorted-key, whitespace-deterministic serialization. Hash gates the fast-path check; the stored manifest body provides the diff message on mismatch.
- **Kindle-time enforcement, not per-command.** One fetch per workbench session, regardless of how many commands run.
- **Both axes ship together.** The tripwire could in principle ship alone (snapshotting current RBRR names), but the rename is the user-clarity half — shipping only the tripwire keeps "RBRR means live" still false. Ship as one heat.

## What done looks like

- A new depot levied via `rbw-dL` writes its manifest atomically as part of levy.
- The four depot-time values live in `rbrd.env` / `rbrd_regime.sh`; references throughout `rbgp_Payor.sh`, `rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh`, and consumers updated.
- Any command touching a depot reads the manifest at kindle and fails loudly if the operator's local RBRD has drifted from the levied shape.
- RBSRD spec exists and explicitly names RBRD as immutable-after-levy.
- Handbook updates point users at RBRD when they hit the relevy-required error.
- The remaining eight RBRR settings stay in RBRR untouched; nothing about live-regime behavior changes.

## Open questions (slate-time, not now)

- Should `RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID` also be captured in the manifest? It's fixed at levy too, but lives in the payor-identity category rather than depot-shape.
- For non-payor commands (director, retriever) the relevy authority isn't theirs — what does the failure message say in those contexts?
- The conversion-from-old-depot heat: is its mechanism just "unmake + relevy" with manual artifact carryover, or does it warrant tooling? Defer until a real case forces the question.

lines: - **Burn the bridges — no migration.** RBRD applies to new depots only. Existing depots remain on the pre-RBRD code path until decommissioned. No dual-read, no compatibility shim, no `RBRR_` fallback for the four relocated names.

- **Conversion deferred.** Migrating an existing depot to RBRD is its own future heat, only built on demonstrated need. The plausible mechanism (unmake + relevy preserving payload) is mentioned here for closure, not scoped here.

-- **Manifest lives in GCS.** Not project labels (charset-restricted, 64-char value cap), not GAR repo description (single-string clobber risk), not Secret Manager (IAM overhead for non-sensitive data). Plain JSON in the depot's own bucket.

+- **Manifest substrate undecided.** At mount-time, choose between a GCS object in a non-build bucket and per-variable artifacts under `rbi_df` (the GAR depot-facts namespace, added in ₣BO; tagged artifacts persist indefinitely). The original GCS rationale held against project labels (charset/length), GAR repo description (clobber risk), and Secret Manager (IAM overhead) — but predated the `rbi_df` namespace existing as a depot-scoped OCI-artifact home.
- **Hash over canonical form.** SHA256 over sorted-key, whitespace-deterministic serialization. Hash gates the fast-path check; the stored manifest body provides the diff message on mismatch.
- **Kindle-time enforcement, not per-command.** One fetch per workbench session, regardless of how many commands run.

- **Both axes ship together.** The tripwire could in principle ship alone (snapshotting current RBRR names), but the rename is the user-clarity half — shipping only the tripwire keeps "RBRR means live" still false. Ship as one heat.
